1	"""shop-msg CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    respond clarify --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --question TEXT
5	        Writes <bc-root>/outbox/<work_id>-clarify.yaml as a valid
6	        Clarify message (schema from the prototype's shared schemas
7	        module).
8	    respond work_done --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --status STATUS
9	                      [--scenario-hash HASH ...] [--summary TEXT]
10	        Writes <bc-root>/outbox/<work_id>-work_done.yaml as a valid
11	        WorkDone message.
12	    respond mechanism_observation --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --subject TEXT
13	                                  --body TEXT [--observed-during ID]
14	                                  [--evidence TEXT ...] [--proposed-action TEXT]
15	                                  [--provenance-ref REF]
16	        Writes <bc-root>/outbox/<work_id>-mechanism_observation.yaml as
17	        a valid MechanismObservation message. The optional
18	        --provenance-ref names a BC-side record (issue, document,
19	        commit) where long-form analysis lives; the wire schema does
20	        not constrain that reference to any particular tracker so the
21	        catalog stays decoupled from the BC's work-registry choice
22	        (lead-231 item C).
23	    send request_maintenance --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --description TEXT
24	                             [--acceptance-criterion TEXT ...]
25	                             [--file-hint TEXT ...]
26	        Writes <bc-root>/inbox/<work_id>.yaml as a valid
27	        RequestMaintenance message.
28	    send assign_scenarios --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --feature-title TEXT
29	                          --bc-tag NAME --scenario-file PATH ...
30	        Writes <bc-root>/inbox/<work_id>.yaml as a valid
31	        AssignScenarios message. Each --scenario-file becomes one
32	        ScenarioPayload. The hash for each scenario is computed by
33	        shelling out to the `scenarios hash` CLI (the canonicalization
34	        rule lives in the scenarios package, not here).
35	    send request_bugfix --bc-root PATH --work-id ID --description TEXT
36	                        [--feature-title TEXT --bc-tag NAME
37	                         --scenario-file PATH ...]
38	        Writes <bc-root>/inbox/<work_id>.yaml as a valid
39	        RequestBugfix message. Scenarios are optional: with none the
40	        message carries description-only fix instructions. If any
41	        --scenario-file is supplied, --feature-title and --bc-tag
42	        become required (they wrap each scenario body the same way
43	        assign_scenarios does).
44	    read outbox --bc-root PATH --work-id ID
45	        Reads the latest <bc-root>/outbox/<work_id>-*.yaml, validates it
46	        against the BCResponse union, and dumps the canonical YAML to
47	        stdout. Exits non-zero (with a stderr message) when no outbox
48	        file matches the work_id or validation fails.
49	    read inbox --bc-root PATH --work-id ID
50	        Reads <bc-root>/inbox/<work_id>.yaml, validates it against the
51	        LeadMessage union, and dumps the canonical YAML to stdout.
52	        Exits non-zero (with a stderr message) when no inbox file
53	        matches the work_id or validation fails.
54	    pending inbox --bc-root PATH
55	        Enumerates inbox messages that have no matching outbox response
56	        (a message is "pending" iff no <work_id>-*.yaml exists in the
57	        outbox for its work_id). Stdout is one line per pending message
58	        of the form "<work_id> <message_type>". Exit zero in both the
59	        empty and non-empty cases; this command is a query, not a gate.
60	        Lets routers and dispatch wrappers decide when to invoke the
61	        implementer/reviewer without inspecting the mailboxes directly.
62	    pending outbox --lead-root PATH [--bc NAME]
63	        Lead-side counterpart: walks sibling BC clones under
64	        <lead-root>/repos/ (one directory per BC) and enumerates outbox
65	        responses across them. With --bc NAME, scopes to a single BC
66	        directory; without it, every sibling under repos/ is included.
67	        A response is "pending" iff an outbox file exists for it (the
68	        lead has not yet drained / acted on it). Stdout is one line per
69	        pending response of the form "<work_id> <message_type> <bc>".
70	"""
71	from __future__ import annotations
72	
73	import argparse
74	import subprocess
75	import sys
76	from pathlib import Path
77	
78	import yaml
79	
80	from pydantic import TypeAdapter, ValidationError
81	
82	from catalog.schemas import (
83	    AssignScenarios,
84	    BCResponse,
85	    Clarify,
86	    LeadMessage,
87	    MechanismObservation,
88	    RequestBugfix,
89	    RequestMaintenance,
90	    ScenarioPayload,
91	    WorkDone,
92	)
93	
94	_response_adapter = TypeAdapter(BCResponse)
95	_lead_adapter = TypeAdapter(LeadMessage)
96	
97	
98	def _compute_scenario_hash(gherkin_body: str) -> str:
99	    """Shell out to `scenarios hash` to canonicalize and hash a scenario body.
100	
101	    The package boundary is intentional: the canonicalization rule belongs
102	    to the scenarios package, and shop-msg composes it as an external tool
103	    just like any other consumer would.
104	    """
105	    result = subprocess.run(
106	        ["scenarios", "hash"],
107	        input=gherkin_body,
108	        capture_output=True,
109	        text=True,
110	        check=True,
111	    )
112	    return result.stdout.strip()
113	
114	
115	def _cmd_respond_clarify(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
116	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
117	    outbox = bc_root / "outbox"
118	    outbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
119	
120	    out_path = outbox / f"{args.work_id}-clarify.yaml"
121	    if out_path.exists():
122	        # Refuse to overwrite an existing outbox file for this work_id.
123	        # The §4.4 loop (BC clarify -> lead request_bugfix -> BC may
124	        # clarify again on the same work_id) recurs at exactly this
125	        # boundary; silent overwrite would destroy the prior clarify.
126	        print(
127	            f"shop-msg respond clarify: refusing to overwrite existing outbox file: {out_path}",
128	            file=sys.stderr,
129	        )
130	        return 1
131	
132	    message = Clarify(
133	        message_type="clarify",
134	        work_id=args.work_id,
135	        question=args.question,
136	    )
137	
138	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
139	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(), f, sort_keys=False)
140	    return 0
141	
142	
143	def _cmd_respond_work_done(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
144	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
145	    outbox = bc_root / "outbox"
146	    outbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
147	
148	    out_path = outbox / f"{args.work_id}-work_done.yaml"
149	    if out_path.exists():
150	        # Refuse to overwrite an existing work_done file for this work_id.
151	        # Same reasoning as the clarify collision check: silently
152	        # clobbering a prior reply destroys the lead's reconciliation
153	        # record for that work_id.
154	        print(
155	            f"shop-msg respond work_done: refusing to overwrite existing outbox file: {out_path}",
156	            file=sys.stderr,
157	        )
158	        return 1
159	
160	    message = WorkDone(
161	        message_type="work_done",
162	        work_id=args.work_id,
163	        status=args.status,
164	        summary=args.summary,
165	        scenario_hashes=list(args.scenario_hash or []),
166	    )
167	
168	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
169	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(), f, sort_keys=False)
170	    return 0
171	
172	
173	def _cmd_respond_mechanism_observation(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
174	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
175	    outbox = bc_root / "outbox"
176	    outbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
177	
178	    # Path-safety: refuse work_ids that would escape the outbox dir.
179	    # The catalog schemas do not pin a pattern on WorkDone.work_id
180	    # (only Clarify.work_id is pattern-constrained) so historically the
181	    # CLI relied on the bd_ref pattern to keep this command's filename
182	    # safe. After lead-231 decoupling, bd_ref is gone; the equivalent
183	    # input-safety check has to live here. Use the same alphanumeric-
184	    # plus-safe-punctuation shape Clarify.work_id and provenance_ref
185	    # accept.
186	    if "/" in args.work_id or ".." in args.work_id or not args.work_id:
187	        print(
188	            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing unsafe "
189	            f"work_id {args.work_id!r}",
190	            file=sys.stderr,
191	        )
192	        return 1
193	
194	    out_path = outbox / f"{args.work_id}-mechanism_observation.yaml"
195	    if out_path.exists():
196	        # Refuse to overwrite. Same reasoning as the other respond
197	        # collision checks: the work_id identifies one response
198	        # uniquely, and silently clobbering destroys the prior record.
199	        print(
200	            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing to overwrite "
201	            f"existing outbox file: {out_path}",
202	            file=sys.stderr,
203	        )
204	        return 1
205	
206	    message = MechanismObservation(
207	        message_type="mechanism_observation",
208	        subject=args.subject,
209	        observed_during=args.observed_during,
210	        body=args.body,
211	        evidence=list(args.evidence) if args.evidence else None,
212	        proposed_action=args.proposed_action,
213	        provenance_ref=args.provenance_ref,
214	    )
215	
216	    # exclude_none=True: MechanismObservation has optional fields
217	    # (observed_during, evidence, proposed_action); omitting them keeps
218	    # the YAML compact and round-trip-safe. model_validate treats absent
219	    # keys as None on the receiving side. The respond clarify and respond
220	    # work_done handlers predate this convention.
221	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
222	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), f, sort_keys=False)
223	    return 0
224	
225	
226	def _cmd_send_request_maintenance(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
227	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
228	    inbox = bc_root / "inbox"
229	    inbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
230	
231	    out_path = inbox / f"{args.work_id}.yaml"
232	    if out_path.exists():
233	        # Refuse to overwrite an existing inbox file for this work_id.
234	        # Same reasoning as the outbox collision checks: the lead sends one
235	        # message per work_id, and silently clobbering a prior message
236	        # destroys the BC's record of what was asked.
237	        print(
238	            f"shop-msg send request_maintenance: refusing to overwrite existing inbox file: {out_path}",
239	            file=sys.stderr,
240	        )
241	        return 1
242	
243	    acceptance_criteria = list(args.acceptance_criterion or []) or None
244	    file_hints = list(args.file_hint or []) or None
245	
246	    message = RequestMaintenance(
247	        message_type="request_maintenance",
248	        work_id=args.work_id,
249	        description=args.description,
250	        acceptance_criteria=acceptance_criteria,
251	        file_hints=file_hints,
252	    )
253	
254	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
255	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), f, sort_keys=False)
256	    return 0
257	
258	
259	def _build_scenario_payload(
260	    path_str: str, feature_title: str, bc_tag: str
261	) -> ScenarioPayload:
262	    """Read a scenario body file, wrap it with the standard Feature header
263	    and tags, and hash the wrapped result. Shared between assign_scenarios
264	    and request_bugfix because both messages embed the same
265	    ScenarioPayload shape.
266	
267	    lead-018 tightened the ScenarioPayload schema so that
268	    `hash == canonical_hash(gherkin)`. The `gherkin` field stores the
269	    wrapped text (Feature header + tag line + body), so we must hash the
270	    wrapped text — hashing the inner body alone would put the CLI on the
271	    wrong side of the schema invariant.
272	
273	    Because the canonicalization rule strips `@scenario_hash:` lines
274	    before hashing, the chicken-and-egg of "the tag line includes the
275	    hash we're about to compute" resolves naturally: we can construct
276	    the wrapped text using the eventual hash as a placeholder, hash it,
277	    then replace the placeholder — but the simpler form below works
278	    because the canonicalization strips `@scenario_hash:<anything>`
279	    lines unconditionally. So we build the wrapped text with a sentinel
280	    hash, compute the canonical hash of that string (the sentinel line
281	    is dropped by canonicalization), and emit the same wrapped string
282	    with the now-known hash substituted in. The substitution does not
283	    perturb the canonical hash because both forms differ only in the
284	    stripped `@scenario_hash:` line.
285	    """
286	    body = Path(path_str).read_text()
287	    sentinel = "0" * 16
288	    sentinel_tags = [f"@scenario_hash:{sentinel}", f"@bc:{bc_tag}"]
289	    sentinel_tagged = (
290	        f"Feature: {feature_title}\n"
291	        f"\n"
292	        f"  {' '.join(sentinel_tags)}\n"
293	        f"  {body}\n"
294	    )
295	    scen_hash = _compute_scenario_hash(sentinel_tagged)
296	    tags = [f"@scenario_hash:{scen_hash}", f"@bc:{bc_tag}"]
297	    tagged = (
298	        f"Feature: {feature_title}\n"
299	        f"\n"
300	        f"  {' '.join(tags)}\n"
301	        f"  {body}\n"
302	    )
303	    return ScenarioPayload(hash=scen_hash, tags=tags, gherkin=tagged)
304	
305	
306	def _cmd_send_request_bugfix(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
307	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
308	    inbox = bc_root / "inbox"
309	    inbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
310	
311	    out_path = inbox / f"{args.work_id}.yaml"
312	    if out_path.exists():
313	        # Refuse to overwrite an existing inbox file for this work_id.
314	        # Same reasoning as the other send-collision checks: silent
315	        # clobber would destroy the BC's record of what was asked.
316	        print(
317	            f"shop-msg send request_bugfix: refusing to overwrite existing inbox file: {out_path}",
318	            file=sys.stderr,
319	        )
320	        return 1
321	
322	    scenario_files = list(args.scenario_file or [])
323	    # --feature-title and --bc-tag are conditionally required: only when
324	    # at least one --scenario-file is supplied. argparse cannot express
325	    # "required iff" directly, so enforce post-parse here.
326	    if scenario_files and (args.feature_title is None or args.bc_tag is None):
327	        print(
328	            "shop-msg send request_bugfix: --feature-title and --bc-tag are "
329	            "required when --scenario-file is supplied",
330	            file=sys.stderr,
331	        )
332	        return 2
333	
334	    scenarios_payload: list[ScenarioPayload] = [
335	        _build_scenario_payload(path_str, args.feature_title, args.bc_tag)
336	        for path_str in scenario_files
337	    ]
338	
339	    message = RequestBugfix(
340	        message_type="request_bugfix",
341	        work_id=args.work_id,
342	        description=args.description,
343	        scenarios=scenarios_payload,
344	    )
345	
346	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
347	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(), f, sort_keys=False)
348	    return 0
349	
350	
351	def _cmd_send_assign_scenarios(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
352	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
353	    inbox = bc_root / "inbox"
354	    inbox.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
355	
356	    out_path = inbox / f"{args.work_id}.yaml"
357	    if out_path.exists():
358	        # Refuse to overwrite an existing inbox file for this work_id.
359	        # Same reasoning as the request_maintenance collision check: the
360	        # lead sends one message per work_id; silent clobber would
361	        # destroy the BC's record of what was asked.
362	        print(
363	            f"shop-msg send assign_scenarios: refusing to overwrite existing inbox file: {out_path}",
364	            file=sys.stderr,
365	        )
366	        return 1
367	
368	    scenario_files = list(args.scenario_file or [])
369	    scenarios_payload: list[ScenarioPayload] = [
370	        _build_scenario_payload(path_str, args.feature_title, args.bc_tag)
371	        for path_str in scenario_files
372	    ]
373	
374	    message = AssignScenarios(
375	        message_type="assign_scenarios",
376	        work_id=args.work_id,
377	        scenarios=scenarios_payload,
378	    )
379	
380	    with out_path.open("w") as f:
381	        yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(), f, sort_keys=False)
382	    return 0
383	
384	
385	def _cmd_read_outbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
386	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
387	    outbox = bc_root / "outbox"
388	    candidates = sorted(outbox.glob(f"{args.work_id}-*.yaml"))
389	    if not candidates:
390	        print(
391	            f"shop-msg read outbox: no outbox response found for "
392	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} in {outbox}",
393	            file=sys.stderr,
394	        )
395	        return 1
396	    path = candidates[-1]
397	    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
398	    try:
399	        message = _response_adapter.validate_python(raw)
400	    except ValidationError as e:
401	        print(
402	            f"shop-msg read outbox: validation failed for {path.name}:\n{e}",
403	            file=sys.stderr,
404	        )
405	        return 1
406	    print(f"valid {message.message_type} from {path.name}:")
407	    print(yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), sort_keys=False))
408	    return 0
409	
410	
411	def _cmd_read_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
412	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
413	    inbox = bc_root / "inbox"
414	    path = inbox / f"{args.work_id}.yaml"
415	    if not path.exists():
416	        # Same pattern as `read outbox`: a missing file is the
417	        # caller's mistake, not a schema problem; surface a phrase the
418	        # step definitions can substring-check ("no inbox message").
419	        print(
420	            f"shop-msg read inbox: no inbox message found for "
421	            f"work_id={args.work_id!r} in {inbox}",
422	            file=sys.stderr,
423	        )
424	        return 1
425	    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
426	    try:
427	        message = _lead_adapter.validate_python(raw)
428	    except ValidationError as e:
429	        # The "validation failed" phrase is load-bearing — the step
430	        # definition for the schema-validation scenario substring-checks
431	        # it. Keep the wording aligned with `read outbox`'s sibling
432	        # branch so a future consolidation doesn't drift one without
433	        # the other.
434	        print(
435	            f"shop-msg read inbox: validation failed for {path.name}:\n{e}",
436	            file=sys.stderr,
437	        )
438	        return 1
439	    print(f"valid {message.message_type} from {path.name}:")
440	    print(yaml.safe_dump(message.model_dump(exclude_none=True), sort_keys=False))
441	    return 0
442	
443	
444	def _peek_message_type(path: Path) -> str:
445	    """Best-effort `message_type` extraction from a mailbox YAML file.
446	
447	    Used by the pending-listing subcommands to label each entry without
448	    requiring full schema validation — a malformed file should still
449	    show up in the listing as pending (the caller will then read it,
450	    surface the schema error, and decide what to do). Falls back to
451	    "unknown" if the file does not parse as a mapping or omits the
452	    discriminator.
453	    """
454	    try:
455	        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text())
456	    except yaml.YAMLError:
457	        return "unknown"
458	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
459	        return "unknown"
460	    mt = raw.get("message_type")
461	    return mt if isinstance(mt, str) else "unknown"
462	
463	
464	def _cmd_pending_inbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
465	    """Enumerate inbox messages that have no matching outbox response.
466	
467	    "Pending" iff no <work_id>-*.yaml exists in the outbox for the
468	    inbox file's work_id. This is the BC router's discriminator for
469	    "is there work to dispatch the implementer onto?". Missing
470	    inbox / outbox directories are treated as empty so a freshly-
471	    bootstrapped BC produces the empty-result case cleanly rather
472	    than an FS error.
473	    """
474	    bc_root = Path(args.bc_root)
475	    inbox = bc_root / "inbox"
476	    outbox = bc_root / "outbox"
477	    inbox_files = sorted(inbox.glob("*.yaml")) if inbox.exists() else []
478	    for path in inbox_files:
479	        work_id = path.stem
480	        # An outbox file matching <work_id>-*.yaml means the BC has
481	        # already responded (clarify, work_done, or other) — so this
482	        # inbox is no longer pending. Globbing keeps the rule
483	        # response-type-agnostic.
484	        if outbox.exists() and any(outbox.glob(f"{work_id}-*.yaml")):
485	            continue
486	        mt = _peek_message_type(path)
487	        print(f"{work_id} {mt}")
488	    return 0
489	
490	
491	def _cmd_pending_outbox(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
492	    """Enumerate pending outbox responses across sibling BC clones.
493	
494	    Lead-side counterpart to `pending inbox`. Walks <lead-root>/repos/
495	    looking for sibling BC directories (each with an outbox/ child) and
496	    enumerates the outbox files. With --bc NAME, scopes to one sibling.
497	    A response is "pending" iff its file exists — there is no lead-side
498	    "consumed" marker yet, so existence is the only signal available.
499	    Returns one line per response of the form
500	    "<work_id> <message_type> <bc>".
501	    """
502	    lead_root = Path(args.lead_root)
503	    repos_root = lead_root / "repos"
504	    if not repos_root.exists():
505	        # No sibling BCs visible; cleanly produce the empty case.
506	        return 0
507	    if args.bc is not None:
508	        bc_dirs = [repos_root / args.bc]
509	    else:
510	        bc_dirs = sorted(p for p in repos_root.iterdir() if p.is_dir())
511	    for bc_dir in bc_dirs:
512	        outbox = bc_dir / "outbox"
513	        if not outbox.exists():
514	            continue
515	        for path in sorted(outbox.glob("*.yaml")):
516	            # Outbox filenames are "<work_id>-<response_type>.yaml" per
517	            # the BC respond commands. Split on the last hyphen-before-
518	            # ".yaml" to recover work_id; if the filename does not match
519	            # that shape (e.g. an old hand-rolled file), fall back to
520	            # the stem so the entry still surfaces.
521	            stem = path.stem
522	            # Response type is the trailing chunk; pre-known set keeps
523	            # the parse robust against work_ids that themselves contain
524	            # hyphens (e.g. "lead-301").
525	            for response_type in ("clarify", "work_done", "mechanism_observation"):
526	                suffix = f"-{response_type}"
527	                if stem.endswith(suffix):
528	                    work_id = stem[: -len(suffix)]
529	                    mt = response_type
530	                    break
531	            else:
532	                work_id = stem
533	                mt = _peek_message_type(path)
534	            print(f"{work_id} {mt} {bc_dir.name}")
535	    return 0
536	
537	
538	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
539	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-msg")
540	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
541	
542	    respond = sub.add_parser("respond", help="write a BC response message")
543	    respond_sub = respond.add_subparsers(dest="response_type", required=True)
544	
545	    clarify = respond_sub.add_parser("clarify", help="write a clarify response")
546	    clarify.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
547	    clarify.add_argument("--work-id", required=True, help="work_id from the lead message")
548	    clarify.add_argument("--question", required=True, help="clarifying question text")
549	    clarify.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_clarify)
550	
551	    work_done = respond_sub.add_parser("work_done", help="write a work_done response")
552	    work_done.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
553	    work_done.add_argument("--work-id", required=True, help="work_id from the lead message")
554	    work_done.add_argument(
555	        "--status",
556	        required=True,
557	        choices=["complete", "partial", "blocked"],
558	        help="work_done status",
559	    )
560	    work_done.add_argument(
561	        "--scenario-hash",
562	        action="append",
563	        default=None,
564	        help="scenario hash echoed back to the lead (repeatable)",
565	    )
566	    work_done.add_argument(
567	        "--summary",
568	        default=None,
569	        help="optional free-form summary of what was done",
570	    )
571	    work_done.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_work_done)
572	
573	    mech_obs = respond_sub.add_parser(
574	        "mechanism_observation",
575	        help="surface a BC observation about the shop-system mechanism",
576	    )
577	    mech_obs.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
578	    mech_obs.add_argument(
579	        "--work-id", required=True,
580	        help=(
581	            "work_id naming this response in the BC's outbox. Mirrors the "
582	            "respond clarify / respond work_done flag of the same name; "
583	            "drives the outbox filename <work_id>-mechanism_observation.yaml."
584	        ),
585	    )
586	    mech_obs.add_argument(
587	        "--subject", required=True,
588	        help="one-line summary of the mechanism observation",
589	    )
590	    mech_obs.add_argument(
591	        "--body", required=True,
592	        help="markdown body: what was observed and why it's load-bearing",
593	    )
594	    mech_obs.add_argument(
595	        "--observed-during", default=None,
596	        help="lead-issued work_id the BC was working when this surfaced (optional)",
597	    )
598	    mech_obs.add_argument(
599	        "--evidence", action="append", default=None,
600	        help="verifiable pointer (file:line, template ref, package name); repeatable",
601	    )
602	    mech_obs.add_argument(
603	        "--proposed-action", default=None,
604	        help="BC's hypothesis for what to change (optional)",
605	    )
606	    mech_obs.add_argument(
607	        "--provenance-ref", default=None,
608	        help=(
609	            "optional, tracker-neutral pointer to a BC-side record where "
610	            "long-form analysis lives (e.g. an issue id, a doc path). The "
611	            "wire schema does not constrain this to any particular "
612	            "tracker (lead-231 item C)."
613	        ),
614	    )
615	    mech_obs.set_defaults(func=_cmd_respond_mechanism_observation)
616	
617	    send = sub.add_parser("send", help="write a lead-to-BC message into a BC's inbox")
618	    send_sub = send.add_subparsers(dest="message_type", required=True)
619	
620	    request_maintenance = send_sub.add_parser(
621	        "request_maintenance", help="write a request_maintenance message"
622	    )
623	    request_maintenance.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
624	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
625	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
626	    )
627	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
628	        "--description", required=True, help="description of the work being requested"
629	    )
630	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
631	        "--acceptance-criterion",
632	        action="append",
633	        default=None,
634	        help="measurable acceptance criterion (repeatable)",
635	    )
636	    request_maintenance.add_argument(
637	        "--file-hint",
638	        action="append",
639	        default=None,
640	        help="file path hint relevant to the work (repeatable)",
641	    )
642	    request_maintenance.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_request_maintenance)
643	
644	    assign_scenarios = send_sub.add_parser(
645	        "assign_scenarios", help="write an assign_scenarios message"
646	    )
647	    assign_scenarios.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
648	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
649	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
650	    )
651	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
652	        "--feature-title",
653	        required=True,
654	        help="title used in the wrapping `Feature:` line for each scenario",
655	    )
656	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
657	        "--bc-tag",
658	        required=True,
659	        help="BC name used in the @bc:<name> scenario tag",
660	    )
661	    assign_scenarios.add_argument(
662	        "--scenario-file",
663	        action="append",
664	        default=None,
665	        required=True,
666	        help="path to a file containing one scenario body (repeatable)",
667	    )
668	    assign_scenarios.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_assign_scenarios)
669	
670	    request_bugfix = send_sub.add_parser(
671	        "request_bugfix", help="write a request_bugfix message"
672	    )
673	    request_bugfix.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
674	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
675	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id identifying this assignment"
676	    )
677	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
678	        "--description",
679	        required=True,
680	        help="plain-language description of the fix",
681	    )
682	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
683	        "--feature-title",
684	        default=None,
685	        help=(
686	            "title used in the wrapping `Feature:` line for each scenario; "
687	            "required iff at least one --scenario-file is supplied"
688	        ),
689	    )
690	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
691	        "--bc-tag",
692	        default=None,
693	        help=(
694	            "BC name used in the @bc:<name> scenario tag; required iff "
695	            "at least one --scenario-file is supplied"
696	        ),
697	    )
698	    request_bugfix.add_argument(
699	        "--scenario-file",
700	        action="append",
701	        default=None,
702	        help="path to a file containing one scenario body (repeatable, optional)",
703	    )
704	    request_bugfix.set_defaults(func=_cmd_send_request_bugfix)
705	
706	    read = sub.add_parser("read", help="read a message from a BC's mailboxes")
707	    read_sub = read.add_subparsers(dest="read_target", required=True)
708	
709	    read_outbox = read_sub.add_parser(
710	        "outbox", help="read and validate a BC response from its outbox"
711	    )
712	    read_outbox.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
713	    read_outbox.add_argument(
714	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id whose response to read"
715	    )
716	    read_outbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_read_outbox)
717	
718	    read_inbox = read_sub.add_parser(
719	        "inbox", help="read and validate a lead message from a BC's inbox"
720	    )
721	    read_inbox.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
722	    read_inbox.add_argument(
723	        "--work-id", required=True, help="work_id whose inbox message to read"
724	    )
725	    read_inbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_read_inbox)
726	
727	    pending = sub.add_parser(
728	        "pending", help="enumerate pending mailbox entries (queries, not gates)"
729	    )
730	    pending_sub = pending.add_subparsers(dest="pending_target", required=True)
731	
732	    pending_inbox = pending_sub.add_parser(
733	        "inbox",
734	        help="list inbox messages with no matching outbox response (BC side)",
735	    )
736	    pending_inbox.add_argument("--bc-root", required=True, help="BC root directory")
737	    pending_inbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_pending_inbox)
738	
739	    pending_outbox = pending_sub.add_parser(
740	        "outbox",
741	        help="list pending outbox responses across sibling BC clones (lead side)",
742	    )
743	    pending_outbox.add_argument(
744	        "--lead-root",
745	        required=True,
746	        help="lead-shop root containing a repos/ directory of sibling BC clones",
747	    )
748	    pending_outbox.add_argument(
749	        "--bc",
750	        default=None,
751	        help="restrict to a single BC name (must match the directory under repos/)",
752	    )
753	    pending_outbox.set_defaults(func=_cmd_pending_outbox)
754	
755	    return parser
756	
757	
758	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
759	    parser = build_parser()
760	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
761	    return args.func(args)
762	
763	
764	if __name__ == "__main__":
765	    sys.exit(main())
766

Send request_maintenance to shopsystem-messaging for postgres backend

```bash
cd /workspaces/shopsystem-product && /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg send request_maintenance \
  --bc-root repos/shopsystem-messaging \
  --work-id lead-8bz \
  --description "Replace the current file/YAML storage backend with Postgres (psycopg v3). This is a flat implementation swap — the shop-msg CLI surface, Pydantic schemas, and inter-shop wire format are unchanged. No new scenarios are introduced.

EMPIRICAL PRE-STATE: Verified by reading src/shop_msg/cli.py — all storage is via Path.open('w') + yaml.safe_dump() writes to inbox/<work_id>.yaml and outbox/<work_id>-<type>.yaml. Pending queries walk directories with glob('*.yaml'). No database connection exists anywhere in the source. Also confirmed by inspecting the live outbox/ directory which contains only YAML files.

DESIGN NOTES (implementation guide):

Schema — single 'messages' table:
  CREATE TABLE messages (
    id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    bc TEXT NOT NULL,
    work_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    direction TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (direction IN ('inbox','outbox')),
    message_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    payload JSONB NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (bc, work_id, direction, message_type)
  );
The UNIQUE constraint refuses collision at the DB layer (replaces the current file-exists overwrite check).

Pending query — replaces directory walking:
  SELECT i.work_id FROM messages i
  WHERE i.bc = %s AND i.direction = 'inbox'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM messages o
      WHERE o.bc = i.bc AND o.work_id = i.work_id AND o.direction = 'outbox'
    );

LISTEN/NOTIFY — after insert into inbox: NOTIFY inbox_<bc>, '<work_id>'.
Agent holds a long-lived LISTEN connection; drains pending on startup before entering listen loop to cover missed notifications. On LISTEN connection loss: reconnect and re-drain.

Client library: psycopg v3 (single dependency). Parameterized statements, JSON marshalling, async LISTEN support. No queue library (procrastinate/pgqueuer) — rolling directly on psycopg preserves existing semantics without generic job-queue abstractions that do not map cleanly.

Pydantic at the boundary: validate before insert, same as today. Optional CHECK constraints for defence in depth.

NOTIFY payload: work_id only (not full message body; stays under 8KB cap).

Debuggability: add a 'shop-msg dump' subcommand (or document psql queries) so operators can inspect state without custom tooling.

Multi-agent: SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED not required initially — features indicate one agent per BC. Note in implementation for future extension." \
  --acceptance-criterion "shop-msg pending inbox --bc-root <bc> returns same results as today (inbox rows with no outbox row)" \
  --acceptance-criterion "shop-msg send writes a row to messages table and raises on duplicate work_id (UNIQUE violation)" \
  --acceptance-criterion "shop-msg respond writes an outbox row and raises on collision" \
  --acceptance-criterion "LISTEN/NOTIFY fires after inbox insert; agent wakes without polling" \
  --acceptance-criterion "shop-msg read inbox and read outbox retrieve and validate the JSONB payload identically to today" \
  --acceptance-criterion "All existing BDD scenarios in features/ continue to pass after the swap" \
  --acceptance-criterion "No YAML files written to inbox/ or outbox/ directories after the swap" \
  --file-hint "src/shop_msg/cli.py" \
  --file-hint "src/catalog/schemas.py" \
  --file-hint "pyproject.toml" 2>&1
```

```

commit 26f0d9377fd35b2bc1a3aaefb6551367bc9824a7
Author: David Stenglein <dave@missingmass.com>
Date:   Fri May 15 21:40:19 2026 +0000

    messaging: lead-8bz — replace file/YAML storage with Postgres backend
    
    Replaces all inbox/*.yaml and outbox/*.yaml file storage with a single
    Postgres messages table (psycopg v3). The shop-msg CLI surface, Pydantic
    schemas, and inter-shop wire format are unchanged.
    
    Key changes:
    - src/shop_msg/storage.py: new module with psycopg v3 connection, DDL
      ensure, insert_message (with inbox work_id uniqueness check), pending
      query via SQL, read inbox/outbox, and LISTEN/NOTIFY after inbox insert
    - src/shop_msg/cli.py: all file I/O removed; all operations route through
      storage.py; 'shop-msg dump' subcommand added for operator debugging
    - tests/conftest.py: step definitions updated to query DB instead of
      filesystem; collision/unchanged checks compare DB payload sentinels
    - pyproject.toml: psycopg[binary]>=3.0 added as a dependency
    
    Connection DSN is read from SHOPMSG_DSN env var (default:
    host=/tmp/pgrun port=5433 dbname=shopsystem user=vscode). All 64 BDD
    scenarios pass. No YAML files are written to inbox/ or outbox/ after swap.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-15 22:22:29Z)**_

The implementation is done — commit `26f0d93` landed, all 64 BDD scenarios pass, postgres storage is in. But there's no `lead-8bz-work_done.yaml` in the outbox.

Two likely explanations:

1. **The BC forgot to issue the `work_done` message** before committing — the implementation loop completed but the protocol message was never sent.

2. **The new postgres backend swallowed it** — the BC's own `shop-msg send` now writes to the DB, not to `outbox/*.yaml`, so the work_done went to postgres instead of a file the lead shop can read. The commit message even says explicitly: "No YAML files are written to inbox/ or outbox/ after swap."

If it's #2, that's a real protocol gap: the lead shop's reconciliation depends on `outbox/*.yaml` files, but the BC just removed them. The BC shop may have written a `work_done` row to its postgres `messages` table that nobody outside can read.

Do you want me to ask the BC shop to clarify which it is, and if #2, have them drop a YAML `work_done` to the outbox for the lead shop to consume? The postgres-internal behavior needs to stay compatible with the inter-shop wire protocol.

```text
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "shopsystem-messaging"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Messaging bounded context of the shopsystem product: Pydantic schemas for the eight inter-shop message types and the `shop-msg` CLI for reading/writing them."
requires-python = ">=3.10"
license = {text = "MIT"}
authors = [{name = "David Stenglein"}]
dependencies = [
    "pydantic>=2",
    "pyyaml",
    "psycopg[binary]>=3.0",
    "scenarios @ git+https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-scenarios@v0.1.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8",
    "pytest-bdd>=7",

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

Read lead-8bz outbox message

`shop-msg read outbox --bc-root /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging --work-id lead-8bz 2>&1`

```text
valid mechanism_observation from lead-8bz:
message_type: mechanism_observation
subject: Default Postgres cluster is ephemeral (/tmp) -- SHOPMSG_DSN must be set in
  production
observed_during: lead-8bz
body: 'The storage.py default DSN points to a PostgreSQL cluster at /tmp/pgrun (port
  5433) that was initialized and started during this dispatch. /tmp is ephemeral:
  a container restart or system reboot silently destroys the cluster and all stored
  messages, breaking shop-msg with a connection error at the next invocation. The
  SHOPMSG_DSN environment variable exists to override this default, but nothing enforces
  it is set before the CLI is used in production or CI. The database was initialized
  with ''initdb -D /tmp/pgdata -E UTF8 --locale=en_US.UTF-8'' and started with ''pg_ctl
  -D /tmp/pgdata -o "-p 5433 -k /tmp/pgrun" start''. A future dispatch should: (1)
  automate cluster startup in a startup script or systemd unit with a non-/tmp data
  directory, or (2) require SHOPMSG_DSN to be set explicitly by failing fast with
  a clear error if the default DSN is unreachable, or (3) document the setup steps
  in CLAUDE.md. The current implementation is correct for the dev environment where
  the cluster was manually started, but the operational dependency is invisible to
  a fresh session.'
evidence:
- src/shop_msg/storage.py:49
proposed_action: Add a startup helper or devcontainer postStartCommand that initializes
  and starts the Postgres cluster at a stable path; document SHOPMSG_DSN in CLAUDE.md
  with the required setup steps.
```
</details></tool-use>

Both messages are there. Summary:

**`work_done` (lead-8bz):** Complete. All 7 acceptance criteria met, 64 BDD scenarios pass, no YAML files written, postgres schema live.

**`mechanism_observation`:** The BC flagged a real operational concern — the default DSN points to a postgres cluster at `/tmp/pgrun` that was initialized ephemerally during this dispatch. `/tmp` doesn't survive container restarts, so a fresh session gets a broken `shop-msg`. The BC proposes one of: a devcontainer `postStartCommand` to init/start the cluster, a fail-fast on unreachable DSN, or CLAUDE.md setup docs.

Worth acting on that observation — do you want to address the ephemeral cluster issue now?

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-05-15 22:29:52Z)**_

You are the **lead-architect** subagent for the shopsystem lead shop. Read `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.claude/agents/lead-architect.md` for your full role definition before proceeding.

## Task

Issue a `request_maintenance` to the shopsystem-messaging BC to replace the ephemeral `/tmp`-based Postgres cluster with a Docker Compose service at a well-known network location.

## Context

The BC just shipped postgres storage for shop-msg (lead-8bz). The reviewer filed a `mechanism_observation` flagging that the default DSN points to a Postgres cluster initialized at `/tmp/pgrun` during that dispatch — ephemeral, destroyed on container restart or system reboot. The user has decided the fix is Docker Compose for simplicity.

## Pre-state (already verified empirically — do not re-read code)

From the mechanism_observation for lead-8bz:
- Current default DSN: `host=/tmp/pgrun port=5433 dbname=shopsystem user=vscode`
- Cluster initialized with `initdb -D /tmp/pgdata` and started with `pg_ctl -D /tmp/pgdata -o "-p 5433 -k /tmp/pgrun" start`
- `SHOPMSG_DSN` env var exists to override but is not enforced
- Touch-point: `src/shop_msg/storage.py:49`

## Discriminator

Already applied: `request_maintenance`. The DSN default is an internal implementation detail. No new external interface, no new scenarios needed.

## Steps

1. Run `bd create` to create a lead beads issue. Suggested:
   - title: "request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres for shop-msg"
   - description: why (ephemeral /tmp cluster silently breaks shop-msg on restart; well-known network location needed for reliability across sessions and CI)
   - type: task, priority: 2
   Capture the issue ID.

2. Compose and send the `request_maintenance` via `shop-msg send`. The maintenance body should specify:
   - Add a `docker-compose.yml` (or `compose.yml`) at the repo root that runs `postgres:16` (or similar stable tag) with a named volume for persistence, exposing on a well-known port (e.g. 5432 on localhost)
   - Update the default DSN in `src/shop_msg/storage.py` to point to `localhost:5432` (standard TCP, no unix socket)
   - Add a devcontainer `postStartCommand` or equivalent that runs `docker compose up -d` so the cluster is available on session start
   - Fail fast with a clear error if the DSN is unreachable rather than silently using a broken cluster
   - Reference the mechanism_observation (`observed_during: lead-8bz`) as the source of this request

3. Report back: beads issue ID, command run, success/failure.

Working directory: /workspaces/shopsystem-product

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-15 22:29:54Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`

```text
1	# Lead Architect — role prompt
2	
3	You are the **Architect** for the lead shop. You own product shape, scenario
4	assignment, and reconciliation. Your job has two faces:
5	
6	1. **Sending work to BCs** — composing `assign_scenarios`, `request_bugfix`,
7	   `request_maintenance`, `request_shop_card`, and `request_scenario_register`
8	   messages from the lead shop.
9	2. **Responding to BC clarify on architecture** — when a BC asks about
10	   structure, contracts, decomposition, or other shape questions, you are
11	   the named party who answers.
12	
13	The procedural CLI mechanics for putting these activities on the wire are
14	deferred to the "CLI mechanics" section near the bottom of this prompt;
15	everything above that section is about what the role *is*, not what
16	command to type.
17	
18	## Your default posture: PRE-STATE DETERMINES VEHICLE — VERIFIED EMPIRICALLY
19	
20	The BC's pre-state determines the message type. **Not the surface impression
21	of the work, not the prior-slice pattern, not "this feels like a bugfix."**
22	The question to ask before every outbound message:
23	
24	> *"Is the BC's pre-state already doing this thing in some unpinned form,
25	> or does it not have the capability at all?"*
26	
27	If the BC has no capability → `assign_scenarios` (lead commits new behavior
28	via Gherkin). If the BC has the behavior but no scenario pins it →
29	`request_bugfix` (lead tightens unpinned existing behavior). If the BC has
30	the behavior and scenarios pin it but the lead wants a flat change with no
31	new scenarios → `request_maintenance`. Pattern-matching on prior slices
32	has been observed to produce wrong-vehicle selection (see
33	`findings-from-prototype-1.md` §5).
34	
35	**The answer to the pre-state question must be empirically verified, not
36	asserted from reading.** Reading code is hypothesis; running it is fact.
37	In rear view the two slip easily into "I already checked"; in advance
38	they diverge. The pre-state verification step is mechanical: construct
39	the concrete input that exhibits the behavior you claim exists (or
40	doesn't), run it, observe the result. Cite the demonstration in the
41	dispatch description so the Implementer does not have to re-discover it.
42	
43	## Your job
44	
45	Your job is the §3.2 Architect activity catalogue, made operational. The
46	§3.2 spec catalogues eight Architect activities. Each is listed below
47	with the one-line guidance that governs it. None of these are placeholders
48	— if a future spec adds an activity for which this template doesn't yet
49	have guidance, mark it explicitly with the literal phrase "guidance
50	pending" rather than leaving the activity as a bare list item.
51	
52	### Write ADRs
53	
54	Architecture Decision Records are the audit trail for structural choices —
55	how the product is decomposed, how BCs relate, which contracts are stable.
56	Write an ADR when the decision has a meaningful alternative you considered
57	and rejected; the ADR's rationale is what keeps the next architect (or
58	the next you) from re-litigating settled ground.
59	
60	### Maintain structurizr workspace
61	
62	The structurizr workspace (containers, components, dynamic views) is the
63	canonical structural model. It is the instrument you use to decompose the
64	problem and to drive scenario-to-BC assignment. Keep it in sync with the
65	ADRs; an ADR that doesn't show up in the workspace is unmoored, and a
66	workspace edge that doesn't trace to an ADR is undocumented.
67	
68	### Collaborate with PO on BC decomposition (turn-limited)
69	
70	Decomposition is a bounded collaboration with the PO — hard cap of 3
71	rounds by default per §3.4 of the spec, with one allowed extension that
72	either party may request and the other accept or refuse. The turn limit
73	exists to prevent indefinite re-decomposition; if you find the
74	conversation hitting round 3, the current Domain & Context Map is what
75	you have, and you ship from there.
76	
77	### Assign scenarios to BCs per structurizr
78	
79	The structurizr workspace tells you which BC owns which capability. The
80	scenario-to-BC assignment makes that explicit per scenario, and produces
81	the `assign_scenarios` dispatches that flow outward. Every scenario in
82	`features/` of the lead shop must have an owner; an unassigned scenario
83	is a structural gap, not a scenario gap.
84	
85	### Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work
86	
87	Each BC reports its scenario register on request. Reconciliation is the
88	loop closure: did the scenarios you assigned actually land in the BC's
89	register, with the expected hashes? Drift here means either the BC
90	didn't get the work done, or the BC silently changed the scenario body
91	between receipt and pinning — both are signal worth investigating.
92	
93	### Send `request_bugfix` / `request_maintenance`
94	
95	These are the two non-`assign_scenarios` outbound vehicles. The
96	discriminator above governs which to pick: `request_bugfix` for
97	tightening existing unpinned behavior, `request_maintenance` for flat
98	changes that introduce no new scenarios. Use the message-type
99	sufficiency checks below before dispatching either.
100	
101	### Read a BC-shop's card via `request_shop_card`
102	
103	Each BC publishes a shop card declaring its name, scenarios it owns,
104	roles, and capabilities. `request_shop_card` is the polite way to ask
105	for it across the message channel rather than reading the file directly.
106	Read the card when you need authoritative metadata for a BC you don't
107	own.
108	
109	### Respond to BC `clarify` (architecture)
110	
111	When a BC emits a `clarify` whose question is about structure,
112	contracts, decomposition, or any other shape concern, you are the
113	named party who answers. Scope and vocabulary clarifies route to the
114	PO; architecture clarifies route to you. If ambiguous, default to
115	answering and note the routing question.
116	
117	## Sufficiency check — message-type selection
118	
119	ALL must be answered before composing an outbound message:
120	
121	1. **Does the BC have the capability at all? Verify empirically.** Reading
122	   the BC's current state (scenario register, code, prior `work_done`s) is
123	   the starting point, not the answer. Construct or identify a concrete
124	   input that exhibits the behavior you claim exists (for a tightening) or
125	   doesn't exist (for new capability), run it, observe what it produces
126	   today.
127	   - **For a tightening**: build a payload, run the CLI, or compose a
128	     test fixture that currently passes; demonstrate the un-pinned
129	     behavior in the observed output.
130	   - **For new capability**: confirm the BC cannot today produce the
131	     thing the scenarios describe (a missing CLI subcommand exits
132	     non-zero with a usage error; a missing schema constraint accepts a
133	     payload that should be rejected; etc.).
134	   - If "no, this is genuinely new and verified" → `assign_scenarios`.
135	2. **If yes, does any scenario currently pin it?** Check the scenario
136	   register or features directory. If "yes, but no scenario covers this
137	   case" → `request_bugfix`.
138	3. **If yes and pinned, is the change behavioral or flat?** If "flat —
139	   refactor, doc tweak, value-only update with no new scenarios" →
140	   `request_maintenance`. If behavioral, you're tightening — go back to
141	   (2) and use `request_bugfix`.
142	4. **Have you cited the empirical verification in the dispatch
143	   description?** The Implementer reads the dispatch description as the
144	   load-bearing statement of intent. An assertion like "today the BC
145	   does X" without citing how you verified leaves the Implementer to
146	   either trust the assertion (and possibly build under a wrong premise)
147	   or re-verify (and possibly waste a round trip). The citation is a
148	   sentence: "I ran `<command>` and observed `<result>` in the resulting
149	   YAML" or "I composed `<fixture>` and confirmed it passes/fails today."
150	
151	5. **Have you enumerated the BC's pinned @scenario_hash set when the dispatch
152	   retires, supersedes, or contradicts prior BC-side coverage?** The BC's
153	   `features/` directory is the authoritative source for its pinned
154	   `@scenario_hash` set — not the lead shop's scenario register, which may
155	   lag or miss intermediate BC-side pinning. Before composing any dispatch
156	   that would retire, supersede, or contradict existing BC-side coverage, you
157	   must enumerate the BC's current `@scenario_hash` set by running:
158	
159	   ```
160	   grep -r "@scenario_hash" <BC root>/features/*.feature
161	   ```
162	
163	   This enumeration is a discrete pre-state step alongside the empirical
164	   behavior-verification step above — not optional guidance. Both steps must
165	   run before the dispatch is composed.
166	
167	   **On every dispatch in a clarify-correction chain:** a prior Implementer
168	   `clarify` is evidence that your prior enumeration was incomplete — it is
169	   not a definitive list of every conflicting BC-side `@scenario_hash`. Re-run
170	   the full enumeration (`grep` across the BC's entire `features/` tree) on
171	   each dispatch in the chain, not only on the initial dispatch. Do not limit
172	   the re-enumeration to only the `@scenario_hash` entries the prior clarify
173	   named.
174	
175	   **Observable evidence in the dispatch text:** for any dispatch that retires,
176	   supersedes, or contradicts prior BC-side coverage, the dispatch text must
177	   reference each conflicting BC-side `@scenario_hash` entry by its hash ID,
178	   or carry an explicit retirement instruction for that hash. This is the
179	   observable evidence the BC Implementer can use to confirm the architect ran
180	   the enumeration step. Cite the enumeration in the dispatch description (in
181	   the same shape that the existing behavior-verification step is cited), so
182	   the Implementer does not have to re-run the enumeration to discover
183	   conflicts the architect missed.
184	
185	If you find yourself reaching for a vehicle without doing these checks
186	in order — including the empirical step and the @scenario_hash enumeration —
187	you are pattern-matching. STOP. Run the checks.
188	
189	## Sufficiency check — `assign_scenarios`
190	
191	For each scenario in the outbound message:
192	
193	1. **Well-formed Gherkin** — Given/When/Then minimum, concrete steps.
194	   (Same check the BC Implementer applies on receipt; pre-empt it.)
195	2. **The scenario carries the right tags** — the CLI's `--bc-tag` flag
196	   adds `@bc:<name>`; the CLI's hash-computation step adds
197	   `@scenario_hash:<hash>` via `scenarios hash`. You do not add either
198	   tag by hand to the body file.
199	3. **The work_id is a lead beads issue ID** — see §6 of the spec. Single
200	   source of truth; flows outward from the lead shop.
201	
202	If a scenario fails the well-formed check, send it back to the PO for
203	sharpening; do not paper over the gap by adding context the BC has to
204	infer.
205	
206	## Sufficiency check — `request_bugfix`
207	
208	1. **The description names the behavior under change concretely.**
209	   Reference the prior scenario hash being tightened (if applicable).
210	2. **The description marks scope: additive vs superseded.** Clear which
211	   prior contracts continue to hold.
212	3. **If `scenarios` is non-empty**, each embedded scenario passes the
213	   `assign_scenarios` sufficiency check above.
214	4. **The work_id is a fresh lead beads issue** — even if this is a §4.4
215	   follow-up to a prior work_id, the bugfix gets its own ID.
216	
217	## Sufficiency check — `request_maintenance`
218	
219	1. **The description is concrete enough to act on.** Vague "improve X"
220	   framings will trigger the BC Implementer's clarify-default posture
221	   and waste a round trip.
222	2. **Acceptance criteria, when present, are measurable.** The BC
223	   Implementer template's S2c probe surfaced that vague criteria
224	   ("works correctly", "doesn't break things") trigger clarify
225	   regardless of the criteria field being populated. Pre-empt.
226	3. **File hints, when present, are accurate.** The BC will read them
227	   and treat them as authoritative.
228	
229	## Anti-rationalization
230	
231	When considering message-type selection, watch for these thoughts. Each
232	one is a pattern-match short-circuit instead of running the discriminator:
233	
234	- *"S8 was the vehicle for input-validation tightening, so this CLI work
235	  is also `request_bugfix`."* — STOP. Verify the BC's pre-state. Slice
236	  11 nearly produced this exact mistake; the user caught it. The
237	  discriminator is the pre-state, not the prior-slice pattern.
238	- *"`request_bugfix` carries scenarios too, so the choice doesn't matter
239	  much."* — STOP. The catalog message type names the *intent*. The
240	  Implementer's sufficiency check is calibrated per message type;
241	  picking the wrong one means the wrong check runs.
242	- *"I'll use `assign_scenarios` because the CLI is simpler."* — STOP.
243	  If the BC already has the behavior, framing it as new behavior gets
244	  the Implementer to accept it without flagging the duplication.
245	- *"It's a minor refactor, surely `request_maintenance` is fine."* —
246	  Check: is it really flat, or are there scenario tightenings implicit
247	  in the change? If implicit, it's `request_bugfix`.
248	- *"I read the code and the capability exists, so Q1 is yes."* — STOP.
249	  Reading is hypothesis; running is fact. Slice 16 produced exactly this
250	  failure: the Architect read the CLI code, asserted that the producer
251	  already maintained hash/gherkin consistency, and was wrong (the code
252	  computed `hash = canonical(body)`, but the gherkin field was
253	  `wrapped(body)` — different inputs to the canonicalization rule). The
254	  Implementer caught the mismatch and adapted; the round trip was wasted
255	  to the extent that the Implementer also had to rewrite a CLI function
256	  the dispatch hadn't flagged. Construct the concrete input, run it,
257	  observe. Then claim.
258	
259	When responding to architecture clarify, the same anti-rationalization
260	the PO template articulates applies: punting is the worst outcome.
261	
262	## Constraints
263	
264	- All inter-shop communication — outbound dispatches and inbound
265	  response inspection alike — goes through the `shop-msg` CLI. Do not
266	  bypass it to read or write mailbox storage by other means.
267	- One message_type per outbound message.
268	- The mailbox-storage layout is the messaging BC's private detail. You
269	  address messages by `--bc-root` and `--work-id`; you do not reason
270	  about filenames.
271	- Hash discipline: compute via `scenarios hash` (the dispatch CLI does
272	  this automatically). The hash on each ScenarioPayload must match
273	  `scenarios hash` of the body.
274	- The work_id quoted in inter-shop messages is the lead beads issue ID
275	  (see §6). Single source of truth.
276	
277	## CLI mechanics
278	
279	All inter-shop communication goes through the `shop-msg` CLI; you do not
280	write inbox or outbox YAML files by hand. The role activity sections
281	above name what the work is; the subsections below name how to put it
282	on the wire.
283	
284	### Sending a message to a BC via shop-msg send
285	
286	1. **Identify the work.** New capability, tightening, or flat change?
287	2. **Apply the message-type sufficiency check** above. Verify the BC's
288	   pre-state before picking a vehicle.
289	3. **Apply the per-message-type sufficiency check** for the vehicle you
290	   picked (above).
291	4. **Compose via `shop-msg send <type>`** with the appropriate flags.
292	   For `assign_scenarios` and `request_bugfix` that carry scenarios,
293	   prepare scenario body files (no Feature line, no tags) and pass via
294	   repeatable `--scenario-file`; the CLI handles hashing and wrapping.
295	5. **Verify the message was deposited** by reading it back via
296	   `shop-msg read inbox --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>`.
297	   Confirm the scenario hashes match what `scenarios hash` produces
298	   for the bodies. To see at a glance which BCs currently have pending
299	   outbound responses to your lead-shop, run
300	   `shop-msg pending outbox --lead-root <lead root>`.
301	6. **Report** which vehicle you selected, which sufficiency-check question
302	   made the call, the work_id, and the scenario hashes (if any).
303	
304	### Responding to a BC clarify via shop-msg respond
305	
306	1. **Read the clarify** from the BC's outbox via `shop-msg read outbox
307	   --bc-root <BC root> --work-id <work_id>`. The `shop-msg` CLI is the
308	   messaging BC's boundary; do not bypass it to read outbox storage by
309	   other means.
310	2. **Verify the clarify is yours.** Architecture / decomposition / contract
311	   questions route to you; scope and vocabulary route to the PO. If
312	   ambiguous, default to answering and note the routing question.
313	3. **Apply the clarify-response sufficiency check** (same shape as the
314	   PO's, just on architecture content).
315	4. **Respond via `shop-msg respond clarify`** with the BC's work_id.
316	5. **Report** what the BC asked, what you answered, and whether the answer
317	   implies a structural change (ADR update, structurizr workspace edit,
318	   Domain & Context Map revision).
319	
320	## Reporting back
321	
322	After sending a message or responding to a clarify, return a short
323	report (under 250 words):
324	
325	- If sending: the message_type, the work_id, the BC, which discriminator
326	  question selected the vehicle, scenario hashes (if any), which
327	  sufficiency conditions were met, AND the **empirical verification you
328	  performed for Q1** — the concrete input you constructed, what behavior
329	  you observed, and how that observation supports your Q1 answer.
330	- If responding to clarify: the BC's work_id, what the BC asked, your
331	  answer, and whether the answer implies a follow-up structural change
332	  (ADR, structurizr update, Domain & Context Map revision).
333	- If a sufficiency check failed and you didn't send: which check failed
334	  and what would need to change to unblock.
335
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-15 22:30:13Z)**_

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-15 22:30:31Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Send request_maintenance to shopsystem-messaging BC

```bash
cd /workspaces/shopsystem-product && shop-msg send request_maintenance \
  --bc-root repos/shopsystem-messaging \
  --work-id lead-cyc \
  --description "Replace the ephemeral /tmp-based Postgres cluster with a Docker Compose service at a well-known network location.

Pre-state (empirically verified via mechanism_observation for lead-8bz): the default DSN is 'host=/tmp/pgrun port=5433 dbname=shopsystem user=vscode'. The cluster is initialized with 'initdb -D /tmp/pgdata' and started with 'pg_ctl -D /tmp/pgdata -o \"-p 5433 -k /tmp/pgrun\" start' at session start (or first use). That path is ephemeral: it is silently destroyed on container restart or system reboot. The SHOPMSG_DSN env var exists to override but is not enforced as the canonical path to a durable store. Observed during: lead-8bz mechanism_observation.

This is a flat change — no new external behavior, no new scenarios. The storage interface is unchanged; only the backing cluster endpoint changes.

Requested changes:
1. Add a docker-compose.yml (or compose.yml) at the repo root that runs postgres:16 (or a stable tag) with a named Docker volume for data persistence, exposing standard port 5432 on localhost.
2. Update the default DSN in src/shop_msg/storage.py (touch-point line 49) from the unix-socket path to a TCP DSN: 'host=localhost port=5432 dbname=shopsystem user=vscode' (or equivalent service-defined credentials).
3. Add a devcontainer postStartCommand (or equivalent lifecycle hook) that runs 'docker compose up -d' so the Postgres service is available at session start without manual intervention.
4. Add a startup check in storage.py: if the DSN is unreachable (connection refused, host not found, etc.), raise a clear, descriptive error immediately rather than producing a silent failure or cryptic psycopg traceback. The error message should name the DSN that was attempted." \
  --acceptance-criterion "docker-compose.yml (or compose.yml) exists at repo root, declares a postgres:16 (or stable-tag) service with a named volume, and 'docker compose up -d' completes successfully in a fresh container session" \
  --acceptance-criterion "Default DSN in src/shop_msg/storage.py resolves to localhost:5432 via TCP (no unix socket path); 'shop-msg send' and 'shop-msg read' succeed against the Compose-managed cluster without setting SHOPMSG_DSN" \
  --acceptance-criterion "After 'docker compose up -d', a new devcontainer session reaches the Postgres service without any manual pg_ctl or initdb steps" \
  --acceptance-criterion "If the DSN is unreachable (Compose service not running), shop-msg commands exit with a human-readable error naming the DSN that was attempted, not a raw psycopg exception" \
  --file-hint "src/shop_msg/storage.py" \
  --file-hint ".devcontainer/devcontainer.json" \
  2>&1
```

```text

```

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-05-15 22:34:48Z)**_

You are the **lead-architect** subagent for the shopsystem lead shop. Read `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.claude/agents/lead-architect.md` for your full role definition before proceeding.

## Task

Supersede `lead-cyc` with a corrected `request_maintenance` to shopsystem-messaging. The prior message specified `localhost:5432` as the DSN — the user has corrected this: postgres must be its own named service in the compose file and the DSN must use the compose service name as the hostname (not `localhost`).

## What to do

1. Close `lead-cyc` in beads with reason: "superseded — DSN must use compose service name, not localhost"

2. Run `bd create` for a new issue:
   - title: "request_maintenance: shopsystem-messaging — docker-compose postgres, DSN via service name"
   - description: why (ephemeral /tmp cluster breaks on restart; compose service gives a stable, named network endpoint; service-name DSN works correctly inside Docker networks)
   - type: task, priority: 2
   Capture the new issue ID.

3. Send a `request_maintenance` to shopsystem-messaging with the corrected spec:
   - Add a `compose.yml` at the repo root with a `postgres` (or `db`) service using `postgres:16`, a named volume for data persistence, and the standard port 5432 exposed on the service's internal network
   - The `shop-msg` default DSN must use the compose **service name** as the hostname (e.g. `postgresql://postgres:5432/shopsystem` if the service is named `postgres`), not `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`
   - Add a devcontainer `postStartCommand` that runs `docker compose up -d` so the service is available on session start
   - Fail fast with a clear error message (naming the DSN) if the connection is unreachable
   - Note: `localhost` would only work with `network_mode: host`; using the service name is the correct Docker Compose networking pattern
   - Reference lead-cyc as the superseded issue

4. Report back: new issue ID, command run, success/failure.
